subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Copernicus: The Sun at the Center
description: Learn Copernicus: The Sun at the Center in one minute.

hook.text: In 1543, Nicolaus Copernicus published a book that removed Earth from the center of the universe.

buildup.text: His model placed the Sun at the center, with Earth and the other planets orbiting around it.

discovery.text: The heliocentric model simplified planetary motion. Retrograde loops that baffled Ptolemy became simple geometry.

twist.text: Copernicus delayed publication until he was dying. His book arrived on his deathbed—he may never have read it.

climax.text: The Catholic Church later banned his book. It stayed on the forbidden list for over 200 years.

punchline.text: Published on his deathbed. Banned for two centuries. Correct all along.

quiz.question: What did Copernicus propose in his 1543 book?
options: That the Sun, not Earth, was at the center of the planetary system
That the universe is infinite with no center
That planets orbit in perfect circles around Earth
